prose:
Conformational rotation barrier of biphenyl (torsion about the inter-ring C–C bond):

Method provenance
• Property: relaxed dihedral scan barrier height (single dihedral 5-9-11-12)
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding)
• Geometry provenance: input Cartesian coordinates from user-supplied biphenyl.xyz; each of the 24 dihedral grid points (15° spacing over 360°) was individually relaxed with the 5-9-11-12 dihedral constrained (ASE FixInternals driver). All 24 optimizations converged (see integrity check).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: chemkit “conformational-analysis” skill (backend: xtb 6.6 with GFN2-xTB Hamiltonian).
• Convergence & caveats: every scan point converged; no engine warnings reported. The calculation employed the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical method (approximate; energies are suitable for qualitative conformational trends, not high-accuracy thermochemistry).
• Live log: the engine wrote a live transcript during execution (path announced at run start, e.g. scan_20260625-115427.out, available in the working directory).

Result
One rotatable bond (dihedral 5-9-11-12) was detected and scanned.
The highest relaxed scan point (near 180°) lies 2.7113787 kcal mol⁻¹ above the minimum (near 225°), giving a torsional barrier of 2.71 kcal mol⁻¹ at the GFN2-xTB//GFN2-xTB level in the gas phase.

Files: optimized structures along the scan are in …/agent_call_01_live_dih5_9_11_12.xyz; the energy profile plot is …/agent_call_01_live_dih5_9_11_12.png.
